master_row.title: Temporal Displacement Factor
master_row.canonical_statement: The temporal displacement factor $\AlphaT$ is defined by $\AlphaT = \frac{dt'}{dt}$, where $t'$ is the local time measured along a geodesic approaching $p$ and $t$ is the global time parameter.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a limiting process relating γ, T, p.
master_row.dictionary: The temporal displacement factor ((t)) is defined by ((t) = dt'dt), where (t') is the local time measured along a geodesic approaching (p) and (t) is the global time parameter.
master_row.encyclopedia: The temporal displacement factor measures how transformed time stretches, compresses, or shifts relative to ordinary time. Interpretive role: Once normalized, this factor becomes the clock correction that later Hamiltonians, actions, and propagation equations can use whenever they pass through or near the singular anchor. Equation grounding: The local equation anchors are 'latex': "(t)=dt'dt,", 'mathml': '', 'wolfram': '', 'canonical': "(t)=dt'dt,".
